pages.description: Create Brawl Stars names in Bold Script Unicode. Live checker validates ~15 chars, and flags symbols that box out.
pages.sub: Generate bold script nicknames tuned for Brawl Stars — with compatibility checks before you rename.
pages.intro:
Brawl Stars players often lean on Bold Script when they need a name that still reads elegant and creator-friendly in busy UI. Rather than guessing whether a copied tag will fit, you can assemble it here and let Fonted flag anything that breaks Brawl Stars rules.

Spaces are allowed in Brawl Stars display names. Aim for roughly 15 characters including spaces. Club tags show up beside your name in matches, so a readable core word matters more than stacking symbols. Script curves look great in profiles but can crowd narrow rename fields if the base word is long.

How to apply in Brawl Stars: Profile → Settings (gear) → Change Name → paste → confirm in preview → save. Always verify in-game — limits are community-tested and can change with patches.

faq.q: Does Bold Script work in Brawl Stars?
faq.a: Most Bold Script letters paste cleanly into Brawl Stars, though rare symbol blocks and emoji can fail on older phones. Use the compatibility row to catch problem characters early.

faq.q: What is the Brawl Stars name length limit?
faq.a: Players generally report a ~15-character cap. Treat that as a planning guide and verify in the actual rename screen before spending currency.

faq.q: Can I use spaces in Brawl Stars with this style?
faq.a: Brawl Stars generally allows spaces, yet styled text can push you past the practical limit faster than plain letters.

faq.q: Is styled text bannable?
faq.a: Supercell rarely bans accounts for decorative Unicode alone, but offensive or impersonating club tags can be reported. Keep names within Brawl Stars community standards.
